extra.reasoning:
The property requires that setting back_pain=true (other inputs fixed) never lowers acuity. back_pain appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100) IF (fever AND slurred_speech AND confusion AND dyspnea AND vision_loss AND leg_swelling AND sweating AND back_pain) THEN acuity:=self_care. This is the highest-priority rule and it sets the LOWEST acuity, and it can only fire when back_pain is true. This is the monotonicity-breaking interaction.

Counterexample baseline: fever, slurred_speech, confusion, dyspnea, vision_loss, leg_swelling, sweating all true; focal_weakness false.

With back_pain=FALSE: interaction_drop does not match. Matching raise rules include raise_dyspnea (emergent, prio 3), raise_slurred_speech (emergent, prio 3), raise_leg_swelling (emergent, prio 3), plus several priority-2 urgent rules. Highest priority = 3 -> acuity = emergent.

With back_pain=TRUE (the witness assignment, everything else identical): all 8 antecedents of interaction_drop hold, so it matches at priority 100, beating every priority-2/3 raise rule. Winner -> acuity = self_care.

Thus flipping back_pain false->true drops acuity from emergent (rank 3) to self_care (rank 0), which is strictly lower. The monotonicity property is violated. The witness_assignment given is the back_pain=true input that yields the lowered acuity (self_care).
